notes: System consists of ATW-R3210 receiver + ATW-T3201 bodypack transmitter + AT831cH lavalier mic. Receiver ports confirmed from manual (ManualsLib) and multiple retailer specs: XLR3 balanced out, 6.3mm TS unbalanced out, 2x BNC antenna inputs (50 ohm, with 12V DC antenna power), DC barrel power input (12V DC center-positive). Transmitter uses Audio-Technica proprietary cH-style 4-pin Hirose screw-lock connector for mic input — closest enum value is xlr4-dc; actual connector is TA4F/Hirose 4-pin screw-down. IR sync is a sensor window on both receiver and transmitter front panels, not a physical port connector. No USB port on this model. Transmitter is battery-powered (2x AA), no external power connector. DC barrel jack exact dimensions not specified in available sources.

ports.name: Balanced Out
ports.note: XLR 3-pin male balanced audio output on rear of ATW-R3210 receiver

ports.name: Unbalanced Out
ports.note: 6.3 mm (1/4") mono TS jack unbalanced audio output on rear of ATW-R3210 receiver

ports.name: Antenna A
ports.note: BNC female, 50 ohm RF antenna input; provides switchable 12V DC for powered antenna accessories

ports.name: Antenna B
ports.note: BNC female, 50 ohm RF antenna input; provides switchable 12V DC for powered antenna accessories

ports.name: DC In
ports.note: 12V DC center-positive input; powered by included 100–240V AC to 12V DC 1A external switching power supply

ports.name: IR Sync
ports.note: Infrared sync window on front panel of receiver (and transmitter); used to sync frequency settings from receiver to bodypack transmitter; no physical connector — sensor window only

ports.name: Mic In (Transmitter)
ports.note: Audio-Technica proprietary cH-style 4-pin Hirose screw-down connector on ATW-T3201 bodypack transmitter; accepts cH-type lavalier/headset mics (e.g., AT831cH included). xlr4-dc is the closest available connector type; actual connector is a Hirose TA4F-style screw-lock 4-pin
